title: Reference Tables in Body Text (tables-and-captions diagnostic)
purpose: Diagnostic for table-presentation preservation across two tables with mixed-content cells (text, bold headers, integers, decimal numbers, inline math), captions, and inline cross-references. Builds on Sprint 3's formatted-text-in-context finding (MinerU emits HTML <table> rather than markdown table syntax) by adding caption preservation and cross-reference resolution. Source uses topical, leak-free section titles (First Three Elements, Astronomical Distances, Glossary) and content-bearing prose that frames the tables without describing their structure.

table_1.caption: Atomic properties of the first three elements.
table_1.label: tab:elements

table_2.caption: Approximate distances of selected solar-system objects.
table_2.label: tab:distances

structure.cross_references: Table~\ref{tab:elements} appears twice; Table~\ref{tab:distances} appears twice
structure.glossary_anchor: Section 3 lists all element names, symbols, numbers, planet names, and AU distances in plain prose; also notes that two captioned tables appear

ground_truth_basis: source.tex (deterministic) + visual spot-check of source.pdf (verified 2026-04-22). Body prose names the topic ('lightest three elements... appear at the top of the first row') without describing tabular structure; the Glossary section anchors content-presence by listing all values plain.

files.probes.json: 12 probes: 2 paired content/format tables + bold + numeric + math + 2 captions + 1 cross-ref + 2 hallucination
